expected.observable_behavior: Equal strings compare equal; different case strings compare not equal (even with case_sensitive=false, source expects false because the library's case-insensitive mode is not implemented in target? Actually source test expects both true and false? Wait source test: TEST_ASSERT_FALSE for compare_from_string("\"ABCDEFG\"", "\"abcdefg\"", true); and also TEST_ASSERT_FALSE for case_sensitive=false? Source test has both true and false? Looking at source: it has TEST_ASSERT_TRUE for both case_sensitive true/false for same case strings, then TEST_ASSERT_FALSE for different case strings with case_sensitive true and also false? Actually the source test: TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(compare_from_string("\"abcdefg\"", "\"abcdefg\"", true)); TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(compare_from_string("\"abcdefg\"", "\"abcdefg\"", false)); TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(compare_from_string("\"ABCDEFG\"", "\"abcdefg\"", true)); TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(compare_from_string("\"ABCDEFG\"", "\"abcdefg\"", false)); So even with case_sensitive false, it still returns FALSE because the strings are different? That seems odd; maybe the library's case-insensitive mode only affects keys, not string values? Actually cJSON_Compare's case_sensitive parameter: if false, string comparisons are case-insensitive? The documentation? Not sure. The source test expects false even for case-insensitive comparing "ABCDEFG" vs "abcdefg". That suggests case-insensitive mode does not apply to string values? Or maybe it does but the test expects false? Wait, the test asserts FALSE, so the library returns false for that comparison even with case_sensitive=false. So we follow source: both comparisons are false. In Rust, eq is case-sensitive, so both comparisons are false. So we can write assert_ne! for both cases. That matches source. Good.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot replicate allocation failure hook mechanism in target Rust crate
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test relies on cJSON_InitHooks(&failing_hooks) to simulate memory allocation failure, which has no equivalent in the target json crate. The target insert operation cannot be made to fail on allocation.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot replicate null pointer arguments in safe Rust
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test passes NULL as root or key to cJSON_AddArrayToObject. Rust's safe type system prevents null references; calling insert on a non-object value returns an error but does not match the specific null-check behavior. The null key case has no safe equivalent.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot replicate allocation failure hook mechanism in target Rust crate
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test uses cJSON_InitHooks(&failing_hooks) to trigger allocation failure; no equivalent in the target crate.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot replicate null pointer arguments in safe Rust
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test passes NULL as root or key to cJSON_AddBoolToObject; safe Rust cannot represent null pointers. Partial coverage (non-object insertion) does not match the exact null-check expected behavior.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot replicate allocation failure hook mechanism in target Rust crate
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test uses cJSON_InitHooks(&failing_hooks); no equivalent in target.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot replicate null pointer arguments in safe Rust
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test passes NULL as root or key to cJSON_AddFalseToObject; safe Rust cannot represent null pointers.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot simulate allocation failure
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test uses cJSON_InitHooks(&failing_hooks) to make allocations fail. The target crate has no equivalent global memory hook mechanism. Cannot replicate allocation failure scenario using available aligned target APIs.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot pass null pointers
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test passes NULL as the object pointer or NULL as the key string to AddNullToObject. The target Rust APIs require valid references for the object and key; there is no way to pass null pointers. Cannot replicate null pointer handling.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot simulate allocation failure
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test uses cJSON_InitHooks(&failing_hooks) to make allocations fail. The target crate has no equivalent global memory hook mechanism. Cannot replicate allocation failure scenario using available aligned target APIs.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot pass null pointers
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test passes NULL as the object pointer or NULL as the key string to AddNumberToObject. The target Rust APIs require valid references for the object and key; there is no way to pass null pointers. Cannot replicate null pointer handling.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot simulate allocation failure
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test uses cJSON_InitHooks(&failing_hooks) to make allocations fail. The target crate has no equivalent global memory hook mechanism. Cannot replicate allocation failure scenario using available aligned target APIs.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot pass null pointers
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test passes NULL as the object pointer or NULL as the key string to AddObjectToObject. The target Rust APIs require valid references for the object and key; there is no way to pass null pointers. Cannot replicate null pointer handling.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot simulate allocation failure
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test uses cJSON_InitHooks(&failing_hooks) to make allocations fail. The target crate has no equivalent global memory hook mechanism. Cannot replicate allocation failure scenario using available aligned target APIs.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Aligned source functions do not include the function under test (cJSON_AddRawToObject), making it impossible to reproduce the expected null-pointer failure behavior without a corresponding target API.
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The case's aligned_source_functions only contain cJSON_CreateObject, but the test predominantly tests cJSON_AddRawToObject with null arguments. No target API exists to add raw JSON content to an object, and the allocation failure hooks are not available. Without a target function for AddRawToObject, the observable behavior cannot be replayed.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: The test relies on cJSON_InitHooks to inject allocation failures, which has no target counterpart. The aligned source functions only include cJSON_CreateObject.
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The expected behavior is failure under allocation failure, simulated via hooks. The target API does not support custom allocators or injection of allocation failures, so the test cannot be replicated.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Aligned source functions only include cJSON_CreateObject; the test focuses on cJSON_AddStringToObject with null pointers. No target API for adding strings to objects.
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: Same as case 2, the primary function under test is not aligned. Cannot reproduce null-pointer behavior.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Aligned source functions (cJSON_CreateObject, cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive) do not include cJSON_AddTrueToObject. No target API to add a boolean value to an object.
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The test requires adding a true value to an object, then retrieving it. Without an add_true method, the test setup is impossible.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Relies on cJSON_InitHooks for allocation failure; no target equivalent. Aligned source functions only include cJSON_CreateObject.
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: Cannot simulate allocation failure in target. Test cannot be replayed.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Aligned source functions only include cJSON_CreateObject; test focuses on cJSON_AddTrueToObject with null pointers.
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: No target for AddTrueToObject. Cannot reproduce.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot create an invalid/uninitialized JsonValue in target Rust API. Rust's JsonValue is an enum with fixed variants; no equivalent to a stack-allocated, zero-filled cJSON struct.
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: Source test uses memset to zero a cJSON on stack and compares it with itself expecting false. In target, any valid JsonValue compared to itself with eq returns true. No target API supports creating an invalid state.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot safely attempt to add a JsonValue to itself due to Rust ownership and borrow rules. The source test checks that adding an object to itself returns false, but in safe Rust it is impossible to pass the same object as both the container and the item because insert and push take ownership of the item.
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: Target APIs JsonValue::insert and JsonValue::push require a value that is moved into the container. Self-referencing would require aliasing and mutable borrowing that violates Rust's borrow checker. No equivalent runtime check exists.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Cannot construct a JsonValue with an invalid type flag (e.g., cJSON_Number | cJSON_String). Rust's JsonValue enforces variant discriminant at compile time.
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: Source test creates a cJSON with type = cJSON_Number | cJSON_String (invalid combination) and expects cJSON_Compare to return false. Target JsonValue is an enum with fixed variants; there is no way to create a value with a mixed or invalid variant. The target API cannot express the source scenario without inventing behavior.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Target does not support creating references to array items; internal pointer comparisons not expressible through public API.
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test creates an array reference using cJSON_CreateArrayReference and directly compares internal child pointers and type flags. The Rust target public API provides no equivalent for creating references or inspecting internal pointers. The aligned source functions (cJSON_CreateArray, cJSON_AddItemToArray, cJSON_IsArray, cJSON_IsNumber) alone cannot reproduce the core behavior.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Target does not support creating references to object items; internal pointer comparisons not expressible through public API.
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: Similar to array reference case: the source test uses cJSON_CreateObjectReference and checks child pointer and type flags. The Rust target has no API for creating references or accessing internal links. Aligned functions (cJSON_AddItemToObjectCS, cJSON_CreateObject, cJSON_IsNumber, cJSON_IsObject) do not cover this behavior.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: Rust's type system prevents passing null pointers as function arguments, making direct null-safety checks inexpressible
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test exercises many public API functions with null pointers and expects graceful handling (returning false, null, etc.). Rust's target APIs take references (&self, &str) that cannot be null; the behavior of calling with null is not representable without unsafe code. Functions like cJSON_IsNumber(NULL) cannot be translated because Rust does not permit calling methods on null references. The required internal public substitution (add_item_to_array -> push) is also impacted because push cannot be called on a null array. This fundamental type-system difference prevents faithful replay.

unresolved_behavior_cases.reason: missing source data: merges array not provided
unresolved_behavior_cases.details: The source test loops over an array 'merges' which is not available in the test definition. Without this data, the expected output cannot be derived.
